title: FSRS (Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler) — Anki
notes: Empirically calibrates 21 decay parameters per user from real review data (gradient descent, log-loss). No lifecycle FSM. Signal is binary human recall, not agent task-success. Substrate is flashcard, not named rule record.

title: A technical explanation of FSRS — Expertium
notes: Per-user w20 parameter (personalized decay exponent) fitted from review history. FSRS-6 trained on 700M reviews from 10K users for default values.

title: Collaborative Filtering with Temporal Dynamics — Koren 2010
notes: Fits exponential decay parameters on user preference drift from real rating data via gradient descent. No FSM, no named rule records. Calibration signal is rating prediction loss.

title: Governing Evolving Memory in LLM Agents: SSGM Framework
notes: Weibull decay function proposed for memory relevance with freshness threshold. No formal FSM lifecycle states. Decay parameters not calibrated from deployment data — theoretical only.

title: FadeMem: Biologically-Inspired Forgetting for Efficient Agent Memory
notes: Shape parameters 0.8 (LML) and 1.2 (SML) appear to be design choices (biologically inspired), not fitted from deployment data. No FSM lifecycle states (draft/active/deprecated/archived).

title: Prioritized Experience Replay — Schaul et al. 2016
notes: Priority (TD-error) drives replay buffer sampling. No FSM, no named rule records, no explicit lifecycle retirement. Implicit decay via never-sampling stale entries.

title: ISO 15489-1:2016 Records Management
notes: Defines record lifecycle FSM (create, capture, manage, dispose). Transitions are policy-driven by fixed time windows / legal requirements. No empirical calibration from usage signals.

title: Adaptive Replacement Cache (ARC) — Megiddo & Modha 2003
notes: Self-tunes partition parameter p online (closest to empirical calibration in caching). Binary in/out eviction, no FSM, no named rules.

title: Temporal BM25 / Document Freshness Scoring
notes: Exponential decay on document age for freshness ranking. Empirical parameter selection by grid search. No FSM, no rule records, IR metrics not agent utility.

title: SuperMemo SM-17 / SM-18 Algorithm
notes: Forgetting index framework with power-regression curve fitting per item. No lifecycle FSM. Calibrates retrieval probability decay, not agent rule utility.
